action.reasoning: Observation: GNN training with pure PyTorch GIN (iter 20) produced NaN loss across all 10 epochs on CPU. Analysis: The custom GIN implementation likely lacked normalization (BatchNorm) and node feature scaling, causing numerical instability. Also, edge features were unused, which may lose important information. Decision: Overwrite model_gnn.py with a corrected implementation that includes BatchNorm after each GIN layer, residual connections, proper scatter_add and global mean pooling using pure torch operations, and node feature standardization computed from training subset. Write train_gnn_v2.py to train on 50k graphs with masked BCE loss, save checkpoint with mean/std, and submit eval after 20 epochs. This should give a stable non-NaN baseline and hopefully improve AP above 0.025.
